title: Can You Multitask? The Brain Says No
title_zh: 你能多任务处理吗？大脑说不
intro_zh: 本文约 1000 词，通过日常生活和科学实验探讨了多任务处理的真相，强调专注的重要性，鼓励读者培养深度工作的习惯。

paragraphs.en: In today's fast-paced world, many people take pride in their ability to multitask. They believe that doing several things at once makes them more efficient. For example, a student might check social media while listening to a lecture, or an office worker might write an email while talking on the phone. But is this really a smart way to work? Recent studies show that our brains are not designed to handle multiple tasks at the same time. Instead, what we call multitasking is actually "task-switching" — the brain simply moves quickly from one task to another.
paragraphs.zh: 在当今快节奏的世界里，许多人以自己能够多任务处理而自豪。他们认为同时做几件事会让他们更高效。例如，学生可能一边听课一边看社交媒体，或者上班族可能一边打电话一边写邮件。但这真的是明智的工作方式吗？最近的研究表明，我们的大脑并不是设计来同时处理多个任务的。相反，我们所谓的多任务处理实际上是“任务切换”——大脑只是快速地从一项任务切换到另一项。

paragraphs.en: Scientists have discovered that when we try to do two things at once, both tasks become less accurate and take longer. This is because the brain has limited attention, just like a computer's processor. When you switch tasks, there is a "switch cost" — a delay in reaction time and loss of focus. A famous experiment at Stanford University compared heavy multitaskers with those who rarely multitask. The results were surprising: people who often multitasked were actually worse at focusing and filtering out irrelevant information.
paragraphs.zh: 科学家发现，当我们试图同时做两件事时，两项任务都会变得不准确且耗时更长。这是因为大脑的注意力有限，就像电脑的处理器一样。当你切换任务时，会有“切换成本”——反应时间的延迟和注意力的丧失。斯坦福大学一项著名的实验比较了频繁多任务处理者和很少多任务处理者。结果令人惊讶：经常多任务处理的人实际上更不擅长专注和过滤无关信息。

paragraphs.en: Why is focus so important? When you concentrate on a single task, your brain enters a state called "deep work." In this state, you can produce work of higher quality in less time. This is essential for learning complex skills or solving difficult problems. On the other hand, constant interruption from notifications or other people makes it hard to think deeply. Even brief distractions can pull your mind away and require several minutes to get back on track.
paragraphs.zh: 为什么专注如此重要？当你专注于一项任务时，你的大脑会进入一种叫做“深度工作”的状态。在这种状态下，你能在更短的时间内产出更高质量的工作。这对于学习复杂技能或解决难题至关重要。另一方面，来自通知或他人的持续干扰会让人难以深入思考。即使短暂的干扰也会分散你的注意力，需要几分钟才能重新回到正轨。

paragraphs.en: Many students underestimate the influence of their electronic devices. A smartphone may seem harmless, but studies have shown that even having your phone on the table — with the screen turned off — can reduce your focus and cognition. The brain is constantly fighting the urge to check the phone. This is called "phubbing" (phone snubbing), which not only hurts your own performance but also makes others feel ignored.
paragraphs.zh: 很多学生低估了电子设备的影响。智能手机看似无害，但研究表明，即使手机放在桌上——屏幕关着——也会降低你的专注力和认知能力。大脑一直在抵抗查看手机的冲动。这被称为“手机冷落行为”，不仅损害你自己的表现，也会让他人感到被忽视。

paragraphs.en: Another common myth is that some people are naturally good at multitasking. While there might be slight differences among individuals, the vast majority of people experience similar limitations. In fact, a study at the University of Utah found that only about 2% of the population can truly multitask effectively. The rest of us simply lose efficiency. So, if you feel tired or stressed when juggling many things, that's normal — your brain is working too hard.
paragraphs.zh: 另一个常见的误解是有些人天生擅长多任务处理。尽管个体之间可能存在细微差异，但绝大多数人都有类似的局限性。事实上，犹他大学的一项研究发现，只有大约2%的人能够真正有效地进行多任务处理。我们其他人只会降低效率。所以，如果你在同时处理多件事情时感到疲倦或压力大，那是正常的——你的大脑工作得太辛苦了。

paragraphs.en: What can we do to improve our ability to focus? First, set clear goals before starting a task. Know what you want to accomplish. Second, create an environment free of interference. Put your phone in another room, close unnecessary tabs, and let others know you are busy. Finally, practice focusing for short periods, like 25 minutes, using the Pomodoro Technique. These strategy can help you train your brain to stay on track.
paragraphs.zh: 我们能做些什么来提高专注力呢？首先，在开始一项任务之前设定明确的目标。知道自己想要完成什么。其次，创造一个无干扰的环境。把手机放在另一个房间，关闭不必要的标签页，让别人知道你正忙。最后，练习短时间专注，比如25分钟，使用番茄工作法。这些策略可以帮助你训练大脑保持专注。

paragraphs.en: The benefit of single-tasking goes beyond work. When you give full attention to a conversation, you build stronger relationships with others. When you focus on eating, you enjoy your food more and eat a healthier amount. Being present in the moment is also a key part of happiness. So next time you are tempted to multitask, remember: doing one thing at a time is not only more productive, but also more pleasant.
paragraphs.zh: 单任务处理的好处不仅仅在于工作。当你全神贯注地听一场对话，你会与他人建立更强的联系。当你专注于吃饭，你会更享受食物，吃的量也更健康。活在当下也是幸福的关键部分。所以下次你被诱惑去多任务处理时，记住：一次只做一件事不仅更高效，也更愉快。

paragraphs.en: In summary, while multitasking feels like a superpower, the science shows it is actually a weakness. Our brains are wired for depth, not speed. By reducing distractions and focusing on one task at a time, we can improve our work quality, creativity, and well-being. Let's give up the illusion of multitasking and enjoy the power of concentration.
paragraphs.zh: 总之，虽然多任务处理感觉像是一种超能力，但科学表明它实际上是一种弱点。我们的大脑天生适合深度，而非速度。通过减少干扰、一次专注于一个任务，我们可以提高工作质量、创造力和幸福感。让我们放弃多任务处理的幻觉，享受专注的力量。
